name: Chris Martin
description:
'''Christopher Anthony John Martin''' (born 2 March 1977) is an English singer, songwriter and music producer. He is best known as the vocalist, pianist and co-founder of the [[Rock music|rock]] band [[Coldplay]], which he formed with [[Jonny Buckland]], [[Guy Berryman]] and [[Will Champion]], at [[University College London]]. They rose to global stardom through ''[[Parachutes (Coldplay album)|Parachutes]]'' (2000) and subsequent albums. 

He received seven [[Grammy Awards]] and nine [[Brit Awards]] as part of the band. Coldplay have sold over 160 million records worldwide as well, making them the most successful group of the 21st century. ''[[The Independent]]'' and the ''[[Evening Standard]]'' ranked Martin among the leading British public figures, while ''[[American Songwriter]]'' named him one of the best male singers of the 21st century.
